in_scope_assets: server/ — HTTP/WebDAV server, handlers, middleware, auth
internal/ — core business logic (auth, fs, op, model, conf, db, sign, search, task, offline_download)
drivers/ — 40+ storage backend drivers (Aliyundrive, OneDrive, Google Drive, S3, WebDAV, FTP/SFTP, local, etc.)
cmd/ — CLI entry points and admin commands
pkg/ — shared utilities (http, utils, cron, generic_sync)
out_of_scope: Third-party storage providers themselves (Aliyundrive, OneDrive, Google Drive, etc.) — only the driver/integration code is in scope
Documentation sites (doc.oplist.org, doc.openlist.team, doc.oplist.org.cn) — content only, not the OpenList server
Older unsupported versions (anything before v4.x latest stable)
Self-hosting misconfiguration issues (weak admin password, exposed admin port, running as root, no HTTPS) — these are user-operation issues per SECURITY.md
Denial of Service against public demo instances
Social engineering, physical attacks, and attacks requiring physical access
Issues in upstream AList that were not introduced or modified by OpenList
severity_ratings: Not formally defined. Default to CVSS v3.1 / standard Critical/High/Medium/Low. Prioritize: auth bypass, remote code execution, SSRF via storage drivers, path traversal in file ops, sensitive token/credential leakage, broken access control on WebDAV/API endpoints.
reward_range: Not offered. OpenList is a community-driven open-source fork with no commercial deployment or paid plans (per project disclaimer). No monetary rewards.
notes: OpenList has no formal bug bounty program. SECURITY.md mandates private disclosure via (1) GitHub Private Security Advisory (preferred) at https://github.com/OpenListTeam/OpenList/security/advisories/new, or (2) Telegram contact @OpenListTeam. Public GitHub Issues for security reports are forbidden. Only the latest stable v4.x release receives security patches. Reports must include: vulnerability description and impact, affected versions, reproduction steps, PoC code or screenshots, and suggested mitigation. OpenList is a governance-driven fork of AList (https://github.com/AlistGo/alist) — focus on code unique to or modified in the OpenList fork relative to upstream AList. Primary language: Go (99.1%). License: AGPL-3.0.
